+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: cd80
+title: 'Architecture: consolidate the two "EntityCache" types — one in-memory entity store at the entity layer, kanban-app becomes a thin bridge'
+---
+## What
+
+There are two types named `EntityCache` in the tree, neither of which does the full job on its own. Consolidate them into a single cache that lives in `swissarmyhammer-entity` and is the only in-memory representation of entity state. The kanban-app retains only a **bridge** that subscribes to cache events and emits board-scoped, computed-enriched events to the Tauri frontend.
+
+### The two caches today
+
+**Cache A: `swissarmyhammer_entity::cache::EntityCache`** (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs`) — the proper one.
+
+- Stores parsed, computed-enriched `Entity` objects keyed by `(entity_type, id)`.
+- `load_all(type)` (bulk preload), `get`/`get_all` (O(1) reads), `write` (write-through, bumps version, emits event), `refresh_from_disk` / `evict` (external changes).
+- Broadcasts `EntityEvent::EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` and `EntityDeleted` via `tokio::sync::broadcast`.
+- Paired with `EntityWatcher` at `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/watcher.rs:33` that turns fs-notify events into cache refresh/evict calls.
+- **Not wired into `EntityContext`.** `EntityContext::list` and `::read` hit disk directly; nothing constructs an `EntityCache` in the Kanban data path.
+
+**Cache B: `kanban_app::watcher::EntityCache`** (`kanban-app/src/watcher.rs:174`) — a change-detector misnamed as a cache.
+
+- `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, CachedEntity { hash, raw_fields }>>>`.
+- Keyed by *path*, not (type, id). Stores raw YAML/Markdown field maps, not enriched entities.
+- Built first; predates Cache A.
+- Purposes (important — this is why it exists):
+  1. **Dedupe our own writes vs. fs-notify.** When a command writes a file, `update_cache` is called so the fs-notify event that fires next is suppressed by hash match.
+  2. **Field-level diff from raw file content.** `diff_fields(old, new)` produces `FieldChange[]` for the `entity-field-changed` Tauri event — this is the frontend's contract.
+  3. **Attachment watching.** Watches `.attachments/` subdirectories and emits `attachment-changed`. The entity watcher ignores these.
+  4. **`flush_and_emit` (synchronous post-write).** After command execution, synchronously rescan + diff + emit to the frontend, bypassing the 200ms debounce — avoids UI latency on user actions.
+  5. **Board scoping.** Wraps events in `BoardWatchEvent { event, board_path }` so multi-window UI filters by active board.
+  6. **Computed-field enrichment hook.** Events are piped through `enrich_computed_fields` (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:2137`) to append `created`/`updated`/`started`/`completed`/tag-derivations that aren't on disk.
+
+Cache B exists because Cache A didn't exist when the watcher was written. Nothing Cache B does is fundamentally about the kanban-app — it's all general "watch the filesystem and notify the UI with rich events." The path-hash map in particular should not be in the app layer.
+
+### Design: one cache, one watcher, a thin bridge
+
+- [ ] **Cache lives in `swissarmyhammer-entity`.** `EntityCache` remains the single source of truth. `EntityContext` gains an `Option<Arc<EntityCache>>` slot (builder `with_cache`) so `list`/`read` consult the cache when attached; `write` flows through `EntityCache::write` (write-through). The path-hash dedupe responsibility moves to `EntityCache`: because writes go through the cache, the watcher fired afterwards finds the hash unchanged in `refresh_from_disk` and emits nothing — dedupe is implicit, no parallel `update_cache` needed.
+- [ ] **Events become field-granular at the source.** Replace `EntityEvent::EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version }` with `EntityChanged { entity_type, id, version, changes: Vec<FieldChange> }`, where `FieldChange { field, value }` matches today's kanban-app type. `EntityCache::write` and `refresh_from_disk` compute the diff between the pre-write/pre-refresh cached entity and the new one — the cache already holds both halves, this is where the diff logically belongs. The frontend contract (`entity-field-changed` carrying a `Vec<FieldChange>`) is preserved exactly; only the producer moves.
+- [ ] **`EntityWatcher` absorbs attachment watching.** Attachments are files under `.attachments/` next to entity files, so watching them is a filesystem concern and belongs in the entity-crate watcher. Add an `AttachmentEvent { entity_type, filename, removed }` variant to `EntityEvent` and handle attachment paths in `parse_entity_path` / `handle_file_event` at `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/watcher.rs:123, 155`.
+- [ ] **`kanban-app/src/watcher.rs` collapses to a bridge.** Delete the path-hash `EntityCache` type (`:174`), `new_entity_cache` (`:181`), `cache_file`, `update_cache`, `resolve_change`, `resolve_removal`, `flush_and_emit`, `diff_fields`, `read_entity_fields_from_disk`, and the in-file hash/cache plumbing. Keep only: (a) `BoardWatchEvent` — board-path wrapper, purely an app concern; (b) a bridge task that `subscribe()`s to `EntityCache` events, optionally applies `enrich_computed_fields` (or that moves into the cache via `ComputeEngine` too), and emits via Tauri. `sync_search_index` (`:120`) also moves to subscribe from the cache. Expected deletion: ~800 of the current ~1200 lines in that file.
+- [ ] **Kill the post-write `flush_and_emit` pattern.** Once writes go through `EntityCache::write`, the event fires synchronously from the write call itself — no "synchronously rescan to beat the debounce" hack needed. The commands.rs call sites that invoke `flush_and_emit` after a command dispatch go away.
+- [ ] **Computed-field enrichment lives with the cache.** `EntityContext::list` already runs compute on load; `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` calls `EntityContext::read` which also runs compute. So cached entities are already enriched. The only gap is that today's `enrich_computed_fields` also enriches the *event* payload with computed values that weren't on disk but changed derivatively. With cache-sourced events that hold the full post-change entity, we can compute FieldChange deltas for computed fields the same way we compute them for stored fields — in the cache's diff step. Move that logic into `EntityCache::diff_for_event`.
+
+### Expected shape after consolidation
+
+```
+swissarmyhammer-entity/
+  cache.rs       ← EntityCache: the only in-memory entity store, emits FieldChange-granular events
+  watcher.rs     ← EntityWatcher + AttachmentWatcher: the only fs watcher, drives cache refresh/evict
+  context.rs     ← EntityContext with Option<Arc<EntityCache>>; list/read/write go through cache
+
+swissarmyhammer-kanban/
+  context.rs     ← KanbanContext constructs one EntityCache, loads all, shares with EntityContext and watcher
+
+kanban-app/
+  state.rs       ← holds Arc<EntityCache>, Arc<EntityWatcher>, bridge subscriber task
+  watcher.rs     ← BoardWatchEvent + bridge: subscribes to EntityCache events, scopes to board_path, emits via Tauri
+                   (path-hash cache, diff_fields, flush_and_emit, enrich_computed_fields all gone)
+```
+
+### Why this is the right level
+
+"Cache entities in memory and refresh on write or file change" is an entity-layer responsibility. Every consumer — kanban app, MCP server, CLI, code-context tools — benefits from O(1) reads and event-driven updates. The kanban-app had to invent its own path-hash cache because the entity layer didn't offer the primitives; now it does, so the app's cache becomes redundant. Board-path scoping and Tauri emission are the only app-level concerns and stay in the app as a bridge.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] One `EntityCache` type in the workspace. `grep -R 'struct EntityCache\|type EntityCache' kanban-app swissarmyhammer-entity swissarmyhammer-kanban` returns exactly one definition — in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs`.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/src/watcher.rs` is reduced to the bridge (target: <400 lines, down from ~1200). It no longer defines `EntityCache`, `CachedEntity`, `new_entity_cache`, `update_cache`, `cache_file`, `resolve_change`, `resolve_removal`, `flush_and_emit`, `diff_fields`, or `read_entity_fields_from_disk`.
+- [ ] `EntityContext::list` and `::read` go through `EntityCache::get_all` / `::get` when a cache is attached — verified by a test that counts `io::read_entity_dir` calls.
+- [ ] `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carries `Vec<FieldChange>`. The frontend's `entity-field-changed` Tauri event shape is unchanged (same JSON schema).
+- [ ] Attachment watching produces `AttachmentChanged` events via `EntityWatcher` (not via a separate app-side watcher).
+- [ ] `flush_and_emit` and its call sites are gone; post-command events fire directly from `EntityCache::write`.
+- [ ] End-to-end: `MoveTask::execute` on a 2000-task board runs in <20ms in a bench (same target as `01KP63Z8GGSY3DPRZ4N37PDY0D`) and a full drag-drop hits <300ms wall-clock.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p kanban-app` stays green. Frontend `bun run test` stays green.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` — `test_entity_changed_event_carries_field_changes`: write entity with fields {a:1, b:2}; write again with {a:1, b:3, c:4}; subscribe receiver sees `EntityChanged` with `changes: [{b, 3}, {c, 4}]` and no entry for `a`.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/watcher.rs` — `test_attachment_event_emitted`: create a file under `.attachments/`, assert `AttachmentChanged` is emitted on the cache channel.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — `test_list_goes_through_cache`: build `KanbanContext`, call `entity_context()` once (which `load_all`s), then do 100 `ectx.list("task")` calls; assert the on-disk-read counter is 1 (the initial load).
+- [ ] `kanban-app/src/watcher.rs` — `test_bridge_scopes_events_to_board`: fake an `EntityCache` event, run the bridge, assert the Tauri payload is a `BoardWatchEvent` with the correct `board_path`.
+- [ ] Delete / migrate the current `diff_fields` / `resolve_change` / `flush_and_emit` tests — their coverage moves to `EntityCache::diff` / `write` / `refresh_from_disk` tests.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p kanban-app` — full green.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && bun run test` — frontend tests stay green (event contract unchanged).
+
+## Workflow & size
+
+This is larger than the usual card budget — roughly 7 files, ~1000+ lines changed once deletions are counted. It is **one concern** (one in-memory cache at the entity layer), but the work fans out across three crates. Recommend:
+
+1. Use `/plan` to split this card into the implementation sub-cards, each under the 5-file / 500-line limit. Natural seams:
+   - Sub-card 1: change `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` shape to carry `Vec<FieldChange>`; migrate cache diff.
+   - Sub-card 2: wire `EntityCache` into `EntityContext::list`/`read` and `KanbanContext`.
+   - Sub-card 3: absorb attachment watching into `EntityWatcher`.
+   - Sub-card 4: replace kanban-app's watcher with the bridge; delete the path-hash cache and `flush_and_emit`.
+2. Each sub-card uses `/tdd`: event-shape tests and read-counter tests first.
+3. This card supersedes `01KP63Z8GGSY3DPRZ4N37PDY0D` (the "wire the cache" perf card) — once `/plan` produces the sub-cards, close that one as subsumed and make the drag-perf benchmark a verification criterion on the relevant sub-card.

- [ ] **Kill the post-write `flush_and_emit` pattern.** Once writes go through `EntityCache::write`, the event fires synchronously from the write call itself — no "synchronously rescan to beat the debounce" hack needed. - [ ] **Computed-field enrichment lives with the cache.** `EntityContext::list` already runs compute on load; `EntityCache::refresh_from_disk` calls `EntityContext::read` which also runs compute. ```

swissarmyhammer-kanban/

kanban-app/

"Cache entities in memory and refresh on write or file change" is an entity-layer responsibility. Keep: `BoardWatchEvent`, `WatchEvent` (it's the Tauri payload type — matches `EntityEvent` shape), `sync_search_index` (moves to the bridge subscriber). Add a bridge task that subscribes to `EntityCache::subscribe()`, maps `EntityEvent` → `WatchEvent`, wraps in `BoardWatchEvent { board_path }`, emits via Tauri. Target: from ~1200 lines to <300.
-- [ ] **Delete `flush_and_emit` call sites in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs`.** The write-through cache fires events synchronously; the app never needs to "catch up" the debounced watcher.

+### Expected shape after consolidation

 ```
 swissarmyhammer-entity/
-  cache.rs       ← EntityCache: sole in-memory store; hash dedupe; FieldChange diff; event broadcast
-  watcher.rs     ← EntityWatcher: sole fs watcher; drives refresh/evict; attachment events
-  events.rs      ← EntityEvent { Changed { changes }, Deleted, AttachmentChanged }; FieldChange
-  context.rs     ← EntityContext with optional cache; list/read/write go through it
+  cache.rs       ← EntityCache: the only in-memory entity store, emits FieldChange-granular events
+  watcher.rs     ← EntityWatcher + AttachmentWatcher: the only fs watcher, drives cache refresh/evict
+  context.rs     ← EntityContext with Option<Arc<EntityCache>>; list/read/write go through cache

 swissarmyhammer-kanban/
-  context.rs     ← KanbanContext constructs one EntityCache, load_all on init, shares with EntityContext
+  context.rs     ← KanbanContext constructs one EntityCache, loads all, shares with EntityContext and watcher

 kanban-app/
-  state.rs       ← holds Arc<EntityCache>, Arc<EntityWatcher>, bridge task
-  watcher.rs     ← BoardWatchEvent + bridge only (board-scope + Tauri emit); ~250 lines
-  commands.rs    ← flush_and_emit and enrich_computed_fields call sites removed
+  state.rs       ← holds Arc<EntityCache>, Arc<EntityWatcher>, bridge subscriber task
+  watcher.rs     ← BoardWatchEvent + bridge: subscribes to EntityCache events, scopes to board_path, emits via Tauri
+                   (path-hash cache, diff_fields, flush_and_emit, enrich_computed_fields all gone)
 ```

+### Why this is the right level
+
+"Cache entities in memory and refresh on write or file change" is an entity-layer responsibility. Every consumer — kanban app, MCP server, CLI, code-context tools — benefits from O(1) reads and event-driven updates. The kanban-app had to invent its own path-hash cache because the entity layer didn't offer the primitives; now it does, so the app's cache becomes redundant. Board-path scoping and Tauri emission are the only app-level concerns and stay in the app as a bridge.
